text.title: Embedding Batch + Cache Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules governing batch + cache embedding pipelines. Validator enforces each mechanically.

rule.statement: batch.size MUST be ≤ provider hard limit: OpenAI 2048 inputs/call, Voyage 128, Cohere 96, Mistral 512. Local sentence-transformers limited by GPU memory only.

rationale.source: OpenAI / Voyage / Cohere / Mistral embeddings API docs 2026

rule.rationale: Exceeding the limit returns 400; matching the limit minimizes per-call overhead.

rule.statement: Output vectors MUST be returned in the same order as input texts. When using async parallel batches, results are reassembled by input index before return.
rule.rationale: API itself preserves order within a batch, but async parallel returns batches in completion order; downstream upsert expects input-order alignment.

rule.statement: Cache key = sha256(normalized_text) + ":" + model_id + ":" + dim. NOT just sha256(text). Cache namespace bumps on model/dim change.
rule.rationale: Re-runs after a model swap must miss the cache and recompute; sharing keys across models silently returns wrong-dimensional vectors.

rule.statement: Before any API call, dedup inputs by their cache key. The unique-set is sent; results are fanned back out to the original positions.
rule.rationale: Boilerplate footers, repeated paragraphs, and chat history repeats often hit 30-60% dedup rate; sending unique-only halves the bill.

rule.statement: Per-batch call is wrapped in retry-with-jitter (max_attempts=5, wait=min(2**attempt+jitter,60)). Only the failed batch retries; sibling batches continue.
rule.rationale: Long indexing runs trip 429 intermittently; full-pipeline crash on a single 429 wastes hours of partial work.
